response: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology, or MIT, was founded in 1861 and holds the #1 spot among leading global universities, currently enrolling 11,500 students and employing 1,069 faculty members, with an extremely competitive 3.9% acceptance rate that reflects its widespread academic prestige. MIT charges an annual tuition of $57,590, manages a $24.6 billion endowment to fund its cutting-edge research and campus operations, and counts an extraordinary 98 Nobel laureates among its alumni, faculty, and affiliated researchers.
